Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
ref: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.324, p.99 (1979)
title: Analyzing power and cross-section measurements for 3He(d,d)3He scattering

x4freetext: Analyzing power and cross-section measurements for
3He(d,d)3He scattering

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.324, p.99 (1979)

x4freetext: 3He gas target (purity 99.9%)
Five detectors were mounted on one turntable
segment. A monitor detector on the other side was
used to check the stability of beam position, current
integration and target density.
At medium angles the correction was generally below
0.5%. At some forward angles however, it amounted to
2% for deuterons and 3% for 3He particles, caused by
the strong angular dependence of the lab. cross
section.
Compilation produced by Arzamas RFNC-VNIIEF
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Entry was re-compiled. Subents 003-006
were added.

reacs.QuantHlp: Differential c/s with respect to angle

x4freetext: At medium angles above 5 MeV the scale error
is about 3%, while at lab angles smaller than 25. degr.
or at low energies it may reach 7%.

flag.hlp: Reference to related data compiled in another entry

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Instrum.and Methods in Physics Res., Vol.4, p.53 (1959)

x4freetext: The lab.
differential cross section was obtained from
Silverstein`s formulae.

compNotes: Conversion from C.M. to Lab: successful

x4freetext: The statistical uncertainty of the analyzing-power
measurements is typically below 0.005.
The relative error of the data points is a
quadratic combination of the statistical uncertainty
and of a random error, due to fluctuating systematic
errors such as background subtraction uncertainty,
current-integration repeatability, beam position
stability, spin-angle uncertainty and measurement of
the beam polarization.
The overall scale error comes from
the calibration of the polarimeter and from the
accuracy of other secondary reference points.
